All You Need To Know About Tours in Iceland

On Guide to Iceland, you will find the largest selection of tours in Iceland at the lowest prices guaranteed, making it easier than ever to plan your dream adventure. From activities like ice cave tours to flexible self-drive tours, our extensive range ensures something for every traveler and budget.

All tours include 24/7 customer support and free cancellation with a full refund up to 24 hours before departure, providing you with peace of mind and flexibility.

How to Explore Iceland: Self-Drive or Guided Tours

There are two main ways to explore Iceland: independently on a self-drive tour or by joining guided experiences. Some travelers prefer the flexibility of driving themselves, while others choose structured tours with transportation and expert guides.

Depending on how much flexibility, support, or structure you’re looking for, Guide to Iceland offers several ways to plan and book your adventure in Iceland.

Popular Types of Tours in Iceland

  • Self-Drive Tours: Travel independently with a rental car, pre-booked stays, a detailed itinerary, and optional activities.

  • Self-Drive Activities: Add meet-on-location experiences to your road trip itinerary, such as ice caving, glacier hiking, and horseback riding.

  • Guided Multi-Day Tours: Join structured tours with a professional guide, hotels, transportation, and daily sightseeing covered.

  • Vacation Packages: Bundle your hotels, guided tours, and airport transfers, and get a flexible and customizable pre-planned itinerary.

  • Guided Day Tours: Take part in guided excursions with expert guides, round-trip transportation, and visits to top attractions.

  • Combo Tours: Enjoy multi-experience packages that combine popular tours at a discounted, value-packed rate.

  • Discounted Tours: Access limited-time offers on selected activities, guided tours, and travel experiences across Iceland.

  • Transfers: Book seamless transportation between airports, hotels, cities, and major landmarks across the country.

Top Adventure Tours in Iceland

Iceland is a land of otherworldly natural wonders and thrilling activities, making it a dream destination for adventurers and nature lovers. Here are the top adventure tours that make Iceland unforgettable:

  • Northern Lights Hunting – Witness the spectacular auroras illuminating the Arctic skies during the winter months.

  • Ice Caving – Explore mesmerizing natural ice caves within Iceland's glaciers, available exclusively in winter.

  • Glacier Hiking – Trek across ancient glaciers like Vatnajokull and Solheimajokull for breathtaking views and a truly unforgettable experience.

  • Hot Spring Bathing – Relax in geothermal pools, from the Blue Lagoon to remote natural hot springs like Reykjadalur valley.

  • Road Tripping – Discover Iceland's breathtaking landscapes by driving its scenic routes to encounter waterfalls and other natural wonders along the way.

  • Waterfall Tours – Visit Iceland’s most iconic waterfalls, including Gullfoss, Seljalandsfoss, Skogafoss, and Haifoss for dramatic views.

  • Whale Watching – Spot majestic whales, dolphins, and puffins along Iceland’s coasts in locations like Husavik and Reykjavik.

  • Silfra Snorkeling – Swim between two tectonic plates in the crystal-clear waters of Thingvellir National Park, one of the world’s top diving sites.

  • Volcano Tours – Marvel at Iceland’s active volcanoes, lava fields, geothermal hot spots, and volcanic craters, such as those at Landmannalaugar.

Top Regions to Explore in Iceland

Each region of Iceland offers a different landscape and travel experience, from glacier lagoons and black sand beaches to volcanic highlands and remote fjords. Here are the top tour destinations in Iceland to help decide where to focus your trip:

  • Ring Road Tours – Embark on Iceland’s ultimate road trip, encircling the island and connecting its most famous sights

  • Golden Circle Tours – Discover the classic route featuring Thingvellir National Park, Geysir Geothermal Area, and Gullfoss Waterfall.

  • South Coast Tours – Explore dramatic black sand beaches, glaciers, ice caves, and iconic waterfalls like Seljalandsfoss and Skogafoss.

  • Snaefellsnes Tours – Journey across “Iceland in Miniature,” where glaciers, lava fields, waterfalls, and coastal cliffs meet in one compact region.

  • Eastfjords Tours – Drive along winding coastal roads past quiet fishing villages, dramatic fjords, and peaceful hiking trails away from the crowds.

  • Reykjanes Tours – Uncover a volcanically active peninsula shaped by recent eruptions, dramatic lava fields, and powerful geothermal forces

  • Westfjords Tours – Venture into a remote and rugged region ideal for wildlife spotting, scenic drives, and experiencing Iceland’s most untouched beauty.

  • Westman Islands Tours – Explore a volcanic archipelago rich in history and birdlife, including puffins nesting along dramatic sea cliffs.

  • Highlands Tours – Travel into Iceland’s untamed interior to witness volcanic landscapes, glacial rivers, and the country’s most spectacular hiking trails.

Seasonal Travel in Iceland: Winter vs Summer

Iceland transforms dramatically between summer and winter, offering two distinct travel experiences:

  • Summer Tours: With nearly 24 hours of daylight, lush green landscapes, and mild weather, summer is ideal for road trips, hiking, and exploring Iceland’s iconic attractions. Rivers swell, waterfalls roar, and wildlife such as puffins thrive during this season.

  • Winter Tours: Snow-covered landscapes, icy terrains, and the magical northern lights make winter perfect for activities like ice caving, glacier hiking, and aurora hunting. Winter also provides a quieter, more mystical experience of Iceland’s natural beauty.

Top 5 Best Summer Self-Drive Tours

Most travelers who visit in the warmer months choose summer self-drive tours, which include a rental car, accommodations, and detailed itineraries to guide your journey, with package discounts applied. Here are the top road trip packages among summer travelers in Iceland:

  1. 10-Day Circle of Iceland Self-Drive – Explore every corner of Iceland at your own pace, including glaciers, waterfalls, volcanic landscapes, and black sand beaches.

  2. 2-Week Complete Iceland Self-Drive Adventure – Experience Iceland’s most stunning natural wonders on this epic two-week journey around the entire country.

  3. One-Week Circle of Iceland Self-Drive Tour – A streamlined itinerary covering all major attractions, from glaciers to geysers, in just one week.

  4. 7-Day Summer Self-Drive Adventure – The ideal one-week tour to explore Iceland’s top highlights during the vibrant summer season.

  5. One Week South & West Iceland Self-Drive – Discover the best of Iceland’s South Coast and magical West on this slow-paced yet scenic one-week itinerary.

Top Winter Self-Drive Itineraries

For independent travelers, these winter self-drive tours provide flexibility and adventure, with the added benefit of increasing your chances to see the northern lights by allowing you to chase clear skies and ideal viewing conditions.

  • 7-Day Northern Lights Self-Drive Tour – A full week of winter exploration, including top sights and activities.

  • 6-Day Northern Lights Self-Drive with Ice Caving – Experience Iceland’s winter wonders, including ice caves, glaciers, waterfalls, and the magical northern lights.

  • 5-Day Northern Lights South Coast Tour – Blend aurora chasing with visits to glaciers and waterfalls.

Top Multi-Day Trips in Winter

The most popular guided multi-day tours during winter take travelers on a journey through the south coast of Iceland to the Crystal Ice Cave by Jokulsarlon glacier lagoon.

  • 5-Day Ultimate Winter Package in Iceland – Experience the best of Iceland in winter with ice caves, northern lights, and iconic sights over five incredible days.

  • 3-Day South Coast & Golden Circle Ice Cave Tour – Combine glacier hikes, ice caves, and Iceland’s iconic attractions in this three-day winter adventure.

  • 2-Day South Coast Ice Cave Tour – Explore majestic waterfalls, massive glaciers, and magical ice caves on this compact winter getaway.

  • One-Week Guided Winter Circle of Iceland Tour – A full-circle guided winter tour of Iceland, featuring glacier hiking, waterfalls, ice caves, and the northern lights.

Best Road Trip Activities

Most self-drive travelers enhance their Ring Road adventure with these meet-on-location activities, offering the perfect break from driving:

  • Jokulsarlon Boat Tour (summer only) – Sail among towering icebergs at the stunning Jokulsarlon glacier lagoon in Southeast Iceland.

  • Glacier Hiking at Skaftafell (year-round) – Explore Vatnajokull’s icy terrain with a guided glacier hike in Southeast Iceland.

  • Katla Ice Cave Tour (year-round) – Discover a stunning ice cave by the Katla volcano on the South Coast.

  • Whale Watching in Husavik (spring through fall) – Spot whales Humpback, Minke whales, and more on a boat tour in North Iceland.

  • Snorkeling in Silfra (year-round) – Swim between tectonic plates in crystal-clear waters in Thingvellir National Park.

  • Snowmobiling on Langjokull (year-round) – Ride across Iceland’s second-largest glacier near Gullfoss.

  • Crystal Blue Ice Cave Tour (winter only) – Discover the magical blue ice caves beneath Vatnajokull glacier, a unique experience available only in winter.

  • Blue Lagoon Comfort Admission (year-round) – Relax in the geothermal warmth of the Blue Lagoon, surrounded by Iceland’s snowy winter landscapes.

  • Northern Lights Tour from Reykjavik (winter only) – Join expert guides to chase the aurora borealis at the best viewing spots near Reykjavik.

Top Year-Round Guided Day Tours from Reykjavik

These guided day tours from Reykjavik are available year-round and are perfect for travelers who prefer not to drive and want to use the capital as a base:

  • The Golden Circle Tour from Reykjavik – Visit Thingvellir National Park, Geysir Geothermal Area, and Gullfoss Waterfall.

  • The South Coast Tour from Reykjavik – Explore Seljalandsfoss and Skogafoss waterfalls, Reynisfjara Black Sand Beach, and glacier viewpoints.

  • The Snaefellsnes Peninsula from Reykjavik – Discover black sand beaches, lava fields, volcanic craters, Snaefellsjokull Glacier, and the iconic Kirkjufell Mountain.

  • The Whale Watching Tour from Reykjavik – Spot whales, dolphins, and puffins on a thrilling marine safari.

  • Horse Riding Tour with Optional Pickup from Reykjavik – Ride the unique Icelandic horse through lava fields and scenic countryside landscapes.

  • Inside the Volcano Tour from Reykjavik – Descend into the dormant Thrihnukagigur Volcano for a unique underground adventure.

Summary

As the solar cycle remains at its peak through 2026, now is the perfect time to plan your visit to Iceland for the best chance to witness vibrant northern lights. With the widest selection of local experiences in Iceland, we make it easy for everyone to find the adventure they’re looking for.

Whether you’re chasing the northern lights, exploring glaciers and ice caves, or driving through breathtaking landscapes, there’s something for every traveler in every season. Iceland’s natural wonders await, offering unforgettable moments year-round. 

Frequently asked questions

What are the most popular types of tours in Iceland?
The most popular tours in Iceland include Golden Circle tours and South Coast tours, which showcase waterfalls, glaciers, and volcanic landscapes in a single day. In winter, northern lights tours are especially popular. Visiting the Blue Lagoon and joining guided ice cave tours also rank among the top experiences for travelers.
What are the best tours in Iceland?
The best tours in Iceland, according to other travelers, are:
1. 10-Day Complete Ring Road Self-Drive Tour of Iceland – Travel around the country and see all the most iconic attractions in one ultimate Iceland adventure.
2. 3-Day Northern Lights Self-Drive Tour – A winter favorite combining glacier hiking and South Coast sightseeing while searching for the aurora borealis.
3. Small-Group Golden Circle Tour with Bruarfoss Waterfall and Kerid Crater –  Visit three of Iceland's unique natural wonders in one day with additional stops.
4. Premium Blue Lagoon Tour with Return Transfer from Reykjavik – Relax in the warm, mineral-rich waters of Iceland’s world-famous Blue Lagoon.
5. South Coast Day Tour – Explore dramatic landscapes including Skogafoss Waterfall, Reynisfjara black sand beach, and Jokulsarlon Glacier Lagoon.
6.  Ice Cave Tour in Vatnajokull National Park – Enter a natural blue ice cave inside a glacier, a unique winter-only experience in Iceland’s largest national park.
What are the best day trips from Reykjavik?
Reykjavik is a great base for exploring some of Iceland’s most iconic regions. Here are some of the best day trips from Reykjavik you can take:
1. Small-Group Minibus Tour of the Golden Circle – Visit geysers, waterfalls, and tectonic landscapes on Iceland’s most popular sightseeing route.
2. Small-Group Tour of the Snaefellsnes Peninsula– Discover dramatic coastlines, glaciers, lava fields, and the iconic Kirkjufell Mountain in western Iceland.
3. South Coast Tour from Reykjavik – Explore black sand beaches, powerful waterfalls, and rugged cliffs along Iceland’s southern coastline.
What are the best tour packages in Iceland?
There are several of the best tour packages in Iceland to choose from, depending on the season and how long you plan to stay. Here are three top options:
1. 5-Day Summer Vacation Package in Iceland – One of the best tour packages in Iceland for summer, featuring Jokulsarlon Glacier Lagoon, the Snaefellsnes Peninsula, and extended daylight under the midnight sun.
2. 5-Day Winter Vacation Package in Iceland – Among the best tour packages in Iceland for winter, this includes ice caving, northern lights viewing, and a visit to the Blue Lagoon Geothermal Spa.
3. 2-Week Self-Drive Tour of the Complete Ring Road & the Westfjords – The best tour package in Iceland for a full-country experience, covering major attractions and the remote, scenic Westfjords of Iceland.
What are the best tours for first-time visitors?
First-time visitors in Iceland should consider:
1. Golden Circle Tour from Reykjavik – A must-see route with geysers, waterfalls, and Thingvellir National Park. The experience also includes a guide and departure from Iceland’s capital.
2. South Coast Tour from Reykjavik – See glaciers, stunning waterfalls, and the stunning Reynisfjara Black Sand Beach on a guided tour with pick-up and drop-off in Reykjavik.
3. Northern Lights Tour in Iceland (if visiting in winter) – A chance to see the aurora borealis in the company of expert guides and a small group of travelers.
4. Blue Lagoon Bathing or Sky Lagoon Tour – Enjoy the ultimate relaxation in Iceland with a visit to Iceland’s top geothermal spas.
5. Whale Watching Tour from Reykjavik – See marine life from Reykjavik, including humpback whales and white-beaked dolphins. This exciting wildlife tour is also available in Akureyri and Husavik in North Iceland.
What are the best holiday packages in Iceland for families?
If you're traveling with children, we recommend some of our self-drive tours that give you the freedom to travel from place to place on your own time with pre-booked accommodations around the country. This 1-Week Summer Self-Drive Tour of the Ring Road is ideal for families, and if you want to see the northern lights, we recommend this 11-Day Winter Self-Drive Holiday, which takes you around the island.
What is the best 5-day tour package in Iceland?
The best 5-day tour package in Iceland is this 5-Day Winter Package in Iceland with Northern Lights and Ice Cave Tours. If you prefer to visit during the summer, then this 5-Day Summer Vacation Package in Iceland is the most popular tour you can choose, with visits to Jokulsarlon Glacier Lagoon and the Golden Circle.
What is the best one week vacation package in Iceland?
If you have one week to explore Iceland during the winter, we recommend this 7-Day Northern Lights Winter Vacation Package, which includes ice caving and visiting the Jokulsarlon Glacier Lagoon.
If you're visiting during the summer, check out the 8-Day Summer Vacation Package of the Best Attractions in Iceland. This package includes a trip to the South Coast and exploring the Golden Circle.
What is the best 10-day vacation package in Iceland?
The best 10-day vacation package in Iceland is this 10-Day Guided Package of the Ring Road. If you prefer visiting in winter, this 10-Day Winter Vacation Package to See the Northern Lights is recommended. Both packages highlight the best regions of Iceland, including Reykjavik and the South Coast.
What are the best tour companies in Iceland?
There are many great tour companies in Iceland, big and small, that take visitors to popular attractions around the country.
1. Troll Expeditions is a tour company based in Reykjavik that focuses on small groups and adventurous activities, and consistently has good reviews and happy travelers.
2. NiceTravel was founded in 2012 by three Icelandic families and provides fun, well-organized tours led by friendly and knowledgeable local guides.
3. Reykjavik Excursions is the largest and most prominent travel company in Iceland, with various bus tours to well-known destinations, and they also operate the popular FlyBus airport shuttle.
What types of tours are available in Iceland?
Iceland offers a wide range of tours to suit different interests and travel styles:
1. Sightseeing Tours – Explore famous Icelandic routes like the Golden Circle, South Coast, and Snaefellsnes Peninsula.
2. Adventure Tours – Experience thrilling activities in Iceland, such as glacier hiking in Skaftafell, snowmobiling tour in Langjokull, ice caving tour in Vatnajokull.
3.  Wildlife Tours – Get up close with wildlife on a guided whale and puffin watching tour from Reykjavik and a selection of horseback riding tours in Iceland.
4. Northern Lights Tours – Chase the aurora borealis by bus, boat, or Super Jeep during winter.
5. Self-Drive Tours – Enjoy the freedom of a road trip in Iceland with a pre-planned itinerary and accommodations.
6. Cultural Tours – Discover Iceland’s rich heritage through Viking history, food tastings, and geothermal spa visits.
How do I know which tour is right for me?
The best tour for you depends on several factors:
1. Your Interests – Whether you prefer nature, adventure, wildlife, or cultural experiences, there’s a tour for you.
2. Physical Ability – Some tours, like glacier hikes, require moderate fitness, while sightseeing tours are more relaxed.
3. Time Available – Choose from short day tours, multi-day adventures, or full vacation packages.
4. Season – Summer tours showcase lush landscapes and long daylight hours, while winter tours focus on the northern lights and ice caves.
5. Budget – Group tours are budget-friendly, while private tours offer a more exclusive and personalized experience.
What is the difference between guided tours and self-drive tours in Iceland?
Guided tours provide the convenience of expert guides, transportation, and planned itineraries, while self-drive tours offer more flexibility and the freedom to explore at your own pace.
Are guided tours better than self-drive tours?
It depends on your preferences. Guided tours in Iceland offer convenience, expert insights, and transportation—ideal for those who prefer structured itineraries.
Self-drive tours in Iceland provide flexibility, independence, and the ability to explore at your own pace—perfect for adventurous travelers comfortable driving in Icelandic conditions.
Can I change the dates of my self-drive tour in Iceland after I have booked?
Yes, you can change the dates of your self-drive tour in Iceland, but it depends on tour availability. However, it's important to note that modifying the dates may also result in a change in the overall price.
Do I need to book a tour in Iceland in advance?
It's highly recommended to book tours in advance, especially during the high season (June-August) when they can sell out quickly. Booking in advance ensures that you have a guaranteed spot on the tour and can also sometimes result in discounted prices. Keep in mind that you can cancel most tours for any reason without charge, up until 24 hours before departure.
Where do Golden Circle tours take me in Iceland?
The Golden Circle is Iceland's most famous sightseeing route and comprises three main attractions: Thingvellir National Park, Haukadalur Geothermal Valley, and Gullfoss Waterfall.
Thingvellir is a UNESCO World Heritage site and is located within a forty-minute drive from Iceland's capital, Reykjavik. Haukadalur Valley is home to the famous hot springs Strokkur and Geysir. The route draws its name from the beautiful Gullfoss Waterfall, which translates to "Golden Falls."
What are glacier tours in Iceland?
Glacier exploration is one of the most authentically Icelandic activities available—over 11% of Iceland’s landmass is, after all, covered with ice caps. Glacier and ice cap tours allow you to explore Iceland’s most popular glacier hiking locations—such as the glaciers Langjokull, Vatnajokull, Myrdalsjokull, and Solheimajokull—on a snowmobile, in a super jeep or by foot. There are also numerous tours that take you inside a glacier, either into natural ice caves or a man-made ice tunnel.
What tours in Iceland are good for avoiding crowds?
The best tours for avoiding crowds in Iceland are self-drive tours where you can explore at your own time and pace by visiting popular attractions outside their busiest hour.
This 8-Day Self Drive Tour of the Ring Road takes you around the country, visiting all the major natural wonders and attractions along the coast. Since you're driving yourself there, you can choose to go there when it's the least busy, such as early in the morning or around dinnertime.
In short, the best way to avoid crowds in Iceland is not being part of one.
What is the appeal of snowmobile tours in Iceland?
Snowmobile tours in Iceland balance incredible sightseeing opportunities and adrenaline-fuelled action. Whizzing and carving your way across some of Iceland’s most staggering ice caps, you'll be exposed to incredible views, intricate ice sculptures and crevasses, and you'll experience the incontestable thrill of speed.
Can I go on ice cave tours year round in Iceland?
Ice caves form within the country’s glaciers and melt away during the summer. Most ice caves, therefore, can only be visited during winter with the help of a knowledgeable guide on ice cave tours. At the start of the winter seasons, glacier guides head out into the unknown to locate newly formed ice caves for exploration. The only ice cave that's possible to visit year-round is in Myrdalsjokull Glacier in South Iceland.
Can anyone go on horse riding tours in Iceland?
Horse riding tours are a great way to experience the beauty of Iceland's nature while getting to know the amazing Icelandic horse. Horse riding is available for both beginner and experienced riders. While some tour operators set their age limits, most will allow independent riders from five years old, but young children are normally led along by the tour guide.
Where can I go on whale watching tours in Iceland?
Whale watching in Iceland is one of the most popular travel activities to engage in, thanks to over twenty species of cetacean that can be found in Iceland's waters. Visitors can choose to go whale watching from various locations, most notably from Iceland’s capital city, Reykjavik, from Akureyri in the north, or the charming town of Husavik, which is colloquially referred to as the "Whale-Watching Capital of Europe."
What should I expect on a typical tour in Iceland?
Most tours in Iceland include transportation, a professional guide, and visits to major attractions. Depending on the tour, you may also get hotel pickup, meals, and any required gear, such as crampons for glacier hikes.
Iceland’s weather is unpredictable, and tours run in most conditions—so dress in layers and be ready for sudden changes!
Are tours in Iceland expensive?
Tours in Iceland can be expensive, but prices vary depending on the type of tour and the length of the tour. Some of the most popular tours, like the Golden Circle and South Coast tours, can be done as a self-drive tour, which can be more cost-effective than joining a guided tour.
Should I tip my tour guides in Iceland?
Tipping is not expected in Iceland, as service charges are usually included in the bill. However, it's not frowned upon to tip for exceptional service.
What tours in Iceland are suitable for families with children?
Many tours in Iceland are family-friendly and perfect for travelers with kids. Some of the best options include:
1. Golden Circle and South Coast tours, which feature stunning waterfalls, geysers, and black sand beaches with minimal walking required.
2. Whale watching and puffin spotting tours, offering an exciting way for children to see Iceland’s diverse wildlife up close.
3. Horseback riding on Icelandic horses, a fun and beginner-friendly activity for kids and adults alike.
Some adventure tours, such as glacier hikes and snowmobiling, may have age restrictions (usually 8+ or 12+ years old), so be sure to check the tour details of each tour before booking.
What are the benefits of taking a tour in Iceland?
There are several benefits to taking a tour in Iceland, including:
• Convenience: A tour in Iceland can be a convenient way to explore the country's top attractions. Tour operators provide transportation, accommodation, and other logistics, which can make travel planning easier and more efficient.
• Expert guidance: Tour guides in Iceland are knowledgeable about the country's culture, history, and natural attractions. They can provide valuable information and context about the places you visit, which can enhance your travel experience.
• Safety: Outdoor activities in Iceland can be dangerous, especially for inexperienced travelers. Tour operators in Iceland prioritize safety, and they have experienced guides who can provide safety tips and guidelines.
• Local perspective: Tour guides in Iceland are often locals who can provide an authentic and unique perspective on the country's culture and way of life. They can also recommend local restaurants, shops, and other hidden gems that may not be found in guidebooks.
• Stress-free travel: Traveling in a foreign country can be stressful, especially if you are unfamiliar with the language and customs. Taking a tour in Iceland can provide a stress-free travel experience, as you don't have to worry about transportation, navigation, or other logistical details.
• Social interaction: Tours in Iceland can provide opportunities for social interaction with other travelers. Group tours can be a great way to meet new people and make connections with like-minded individuals.
• Time-saving: Iceland has many natural attractions that are spread out across the country, and it can be time-consuming to visit them all on your own. Tours in Iceland can provide a time-saving option, as they typically visit multiple attractions in a single day or week.
In summary, taking a tour in Iceland can provide convenience, safety, a local perspective, and stress-free travel. It can also offer opportunities for social interaction and time-saving options.
When is the best time of year to visit Iceland?
The best time to visit Iceland depends on your interests. If you want to see the northern lights, the best time to visit is between September and April. However, if you want to experience the midnight sun, you should visit during the summer months (June through August).
The shoulder seasons of May and September can also be a good time to visit, as the crowds at the most popular attractions are smaller.
Are there guided tours available year-round in Iceland?
Guided tours are available throughout the year, with options tailored to each season. Winter tours often focus on activities like northern lights hunting and visiting ice caves, while summer tours highlight long daylight hours, waterfalls, and green landscapes.
Are private tours available in Iceland?
Yes, many operators offer private guided tours in Iceland for a more personalized experience. Private tours are great for families, photographers, or those wanting custom itineraries.
Do I need to rent a car to explore Iceland?
While it is possible to explore Iceland by renting a car, there are many guided tours available that take visitors to popular attractions. For those who prefer not to drive, a guided tour is a good option.
What should I wear on a tour in Iceland?
It's important to dress appropriately for the type of tour you're going on and the weather conditions. In general, it's recommended to dress in layers, including a waterproof and windproof outer layer, warm and waterproof boots, and a hat and gloves. Some tours, such as glacier hiking and ice cave tours, may require additional gear, such as crampons and helmets, which will be provided by the tour company. If you're hoping to dip into a hot spring or a local swimming pool, make sure to pack a swimsuit as well.
What should I pack for a tour in Iceland?
The weather in Iceland is unpredictable, so it is best to pack for all types of weather. Layers are key, and visitors should bring waterproof and windproof jackets, hiking boots, thermal undergarments, gloves, and hats. It's also a good idea to bring a swimsuit for visiting geothermal pools.
Do Iceland tours operate in bad weather?
• Most tours run regardless of rain, wind, or snow—dressing appropriately is key! 
• Tours may be canceled or rescheduled in severe weather conditions (e.g., storms, heavy snowfall). 
• If a tour is canceled, most operators offer full refunds or rebooking options.
How can I prepare for tours in Iceland's unpredictable weather?
It's important to dress in layers and bring waterproof and windproof clothing. Always check the weather forecast and follow the recommendations of your tour operator for specific gear.
How long do tours in Iceland typically last?
Day tours usually last 4–12 hours, while multi-day tours can range from 2 to 14 days. Shorter experiences like whale watching or northern lights tours may only take 1–3 hours.
How long is the perfect vacation in Iceland?
The length of your Iceland vacation depends on your interests and itinerary. A vacation package that takes you around the Ring Road, which circles the entire country, can take around 10-14 days. A shorter trip to Reykjavik and the Golden Circle can be done in 3-5 days.
Can I book tours in Iceland last-minute?
It's possible to book tours in Iceland last-minute, but availability can be limited during peak travel season. It's recommended to book in advance to ensure availability and to secure the best price.
Can I change my tour booking?
Yes, you can change your tour booking by sending us an email or using our chat widget.
Can I cancel my tour in Iceland?
Yes, you can cancel a tour for any reason and receive a full refund if you cancel at least 24 hours before departure.
Are tours accessible for people with disabilities?
Accessibility varies among tours in Iceland. It's important to research and inquire about accessibility options before booking a tour. Some tour operators offer accessible vehicles, wheelchair ramps, and other accommodations for people with disabilities
Are there any age restrictions for tours in Iceland?
Some tours in Iceland have age restrictions due to safety concerns. It's important to check before booking to ensure that the tour is suitable for your age group.
 
Are there any health concerns I should be aware of when participating in tours in Iceland?
Iceland is a relatively safe country, but visitors should be aware of the risks of outdoor activities, such as hiking, snowmobiling, and snorkeling in cold water. Visitors should also be prepared for varying weather conditions, which can be unpredictable.
Are there any cultural norms I should be aware of when participating in tours in Iceland?
Icelandic culture places a high value on respect and consideration for others. It's important to follow tour guidelines, respect the environment, and be mindful of other participants. Tipping is not expected, but it's common to show appreciation for exceptional service.
Are there any restrictions for photography or filming on tours in Iceland?
There are no restrictions on taking photos or videos on tours in Iceland, but in some places, there might be restrictions on flying drones.
Is it necessary to have travel insurance when booking a tour in Iceland?
It's recommended to have travel insurance when booking a tour in Iceland to cover unexpected medical emergencies.
Are meals included in tours in Iceland?
Meals are not always included in tours in Iceland, but many tours offer meal options or stops at local restaurants. It's important to check before booking to avoid any surprises.
Can I bring my own snacks or food on tours in Iceland?
It's possible to bring your own snacks or food on tours in Iceland, but some tours provide offer meal options or may stop at petrol stations where you can have quick meals like Icelandic hot dogs.
Can I customize my own tour in Iceland?
Yes, just send us a message and we'll help you customize your tour in Iceland.
Do I need a tour guide to visit natural attractions in Iceland?
Visitors can visit most natural attractions in Iceland on their own, but it's recommended to hire a guide for safety and environmental reasons. A guide can also provide valuable information and context about the attraction.
Can I book a tour that includes multiple activities?
Many tours in Iceland offer multiple activities, such as hiking, snowmobiling, and visiting natural attractions. It's important to check before booking to ensure that the tour includes the activities that you are interested in.
Can I join a tour in Iceland as a solo traveler?
Yes, many tours are ideal for solo travelers, offering opportunities to meet others while exploring Iceland's attractions with the convenience of a guide.
Are there any special considerations for solo travelers?
Many tours welcome solo travelers, and group tours are a great way to meet others. Some guided tours do not require a single supplement, making them budget-friendly. Self-drive tours are also an option, but be aware of remote roads and winter driving conditions.
What payment methods are accepted when I book my Iceland trip?
You can pay on Guide to Iceland using major credit cards, including Visa, Mastercard, and American Express, as well as Apple Pay, Google Pay, Alipay, and WeChat Pay. You can also choose to pay later or in installments with PayPal or Klarna at checkout.
Do I need to pay my Iceland trip in full at the time of booking?
You can either pay in full at the time of booking or choose to pay later and split the cost into installments with PayPal or Klarna at checkout.
Is my payment secure when booking my Iceland tours online?
Yes, all online payments on Guide to Iceland are processed through secure, encrypted systems to protect your personal and financial information. Industry-standard security measures are used to ensure your transaction details remain private and safe.
Do prices change if I add or remove elements from my tour or package in Iceland?
Yes, the total price will change if you add, remove, or upgrade elements in your package, such as accommodation, rental cars, or activities. Any adjustments are clearly reflected before you confirm changes, so you can review the updated total cost in advance.
When is the best time to book tours and packages in Iceland for the best prices?
The best time to book tours and vacation packages in Iceland is as early as possible, especially for summer travel between June and August when demand is highest. Booking in advance helps secure better availability and pricing, while winter travel often offers more flexibility but should still be reserved ahead of peak northern lights season.
How far in advance should I book tours in Iceland?
It’s recommended to book tours in Iceland at least 2–6 months in advance for summer travel, especially between June and August when demand is highest. For winter trips, booking a few months ahead is still advised, particularly if you plan to join northern lights tours or visit popular attractions.
What is the latest I can book a tour or package?
Many tours in Iceland can be booked up to 24 hours before departure, and some even allow same-day booking depending on availability. Multi-day tours and vacation packages are best secured at least a couple of weeks in advance, as hotel rooms and popular activities often sell out quickly, especially during peak travel months in summer and around the northern lights season.
What is the latest I can book a tour or package?
Many tours in Iceland can be booked up to 24 hours before departure, and some even allow same-day booking depending on availability. Multi-day tours and vacation packages are best secured at least a couple of weeks in advance, as hotel rooms and popular activities often sell out quickly, especially during peak travel months in summer and around the northern lights season.
What is included and excluded in Iceland tours and packages?
Inclusions vary by tour and package in Iceland, but most include accommodation, transportation, and any guided activities listed in the itinerary. Meals, international flights, travel insurance, and optional add-ons are usually not included unless clearly stated on the tour page, so reviewing the full description before booking is always recommended.
Can I see a sample itinerary before booking an Iceland tour or package?
Yes, every tour and package includes a detailed day-by-day itinerary on the product page, outlining activities, accommodation, and key attractions. You can review the full schedule, inclusions, and important travel information before booking to ensure the trip matches your expectations.
Can I see a sample itinerary before booking an Iceland tour or package?
Yes, every tour and package includes a detailed day-by-day itinerary on the product page, outlining activities, accommodation, and key attractions. You can review the full schedule, inclusions, and important travel information before booking to ensure the trip matches your expectations.
Are flights included in Iceland vacation packages?
International flights to Iceland are not typically included in vacation packages unless clearly stated on the booking page. Most packages focus on accommodation, transportation within Iceland, and planned activities, but you can review each package’s inclusions before booking to see exactly what is covered.
What happens if my flight to Iceland is delayed or canceled?
If your flight to Iceland is delayed or canceled, contact Guide to Iceland as soon as possible so your booking can be reviewed and adjusted if needed. The support team will assist with rescheduling airport transfers or tours based on availability and supplier policies.
Is airport transfer included in my Iceland vacation package?
Most Iceland vacation packages include airport transfers, while multi-day guided tours typically do not unless specifically stated. Always check the tour page for detailed inclusions so you can confirm whether airport pickup and drop-off are part of your booking.
What do I do on arrival in Iceland?
After arriving at Keflavik International Airport, follow the instructions provided in your booking confirmation for airport transfer or rental car pickup. If your package includes transfers, you’ll receive details about where to meet your driver or board your shuttle, and if you’ve booked a rental car, pickup information will be clearly outlined in your travel documents.
How does weather affect Iceland tours and vacation packages?
The weather in Iceland can occasionally affect tour schedules, especially during winter when snow, wind, or road conditions may require adjustments. In such cases, operators may modify the itinerary, reschedule activities, or offer suitable alternatives to ensure safety while maintaining the best possible experience.
Are some Iceland tours only available in certain seasons?
Yes, some Iceland tours operate only during specific seasons due to weather and road conditions. Ice cave tours and northern lights tours are available mainly in winter, from October to April. Midnight sun tours and Highland tours run in summer, from June to early September, when mountain roads are open and daylight hours are at their longest.
Are Iceland tours suitable for seniors or travelers with mobility needs?
Many Iceland tours are suitable for seniors and travelers with limited mobility, but accessibility varies depending on the activity and terrain. Sightseeing tours by coach and city experiences are generally easier to access, while glacier hikes or highland excursions may require good physical condition. If you have specific mobility needs, you can contact our travel agents for help finding accessible tours that match your requirements.
Are there Iceland tours for honeymooners or special occasions?
Yes, Iceland offers tours and vacation packages designed for honeymooners and special occasions, including luxury stays, private tours, and unique experiences such as northern lights excursions or geothermal spa visits. You can choose from romantic packages or customize your itinerary to create a more personalized celebration.
Are group discounts or special pricing available for Iceland tours?
Group discounts may be available for larger bookings, private tours, or custom vacation packages in Iceland. Pricing depends on group size and the services included, so contacting Guide to Iceland for a tailored quote is recommended.
What travel insurance do I need for a trip to Iceland?
Travel insurance for Iceland should cover medical expenses, trip cancellations, delays, and lost luggage. While Iceland is a safe destination, comprehensive coverage is recommended due to changing weather conditions and outdoor activities that may involve some level of risk. Always review your policy details to ensure it covers the specific tours and activities you plan to join.
What safety precautions are in place on Iceland tours?
Safety is a top priority on Iceland tours, and licensed operators follow strict local regulations and weather guidelines. Professional guides are trained to assess conditions and provide safety instructions, and specialized equipment is provided when required for activities such as glacier hikes or snowmobiling.
What is the cancellation and refund policy for Iceland tours and vacation packages?
Many Iceland tours and vacation packages offer free cancellation up to a specified time before departure, but policies vary by product. The exact cancellation terms and refund eligibility are always listed on the tour page, so reviewing them before booking is recommended.
How can I contact customer support?
Guide to Iceland offers 24/7 customer support to assist with bookings, changes, or travel questions. You can reach the support team through the contact options provided on the website for prompt assistance.
What is the best-price guarantee and how does it work?
Guide to Iceland offers a best-price guarantee, meaning you won’t pay more than the standard listed price for the same tour or package. If you find the same product available at a lower price under the same conditions, you can contact our travel agents for review according to the guarantee terms.
Can I build a completely custom trip itinerary in Iceland?
All Iceland vacation packages are customizable, allowing you to choose your preferred accommodation, transportation option, and add extra tours or experiences during booking. If you’d like a fully tailored trip, Guide to Iceland’s travel specialists can help design a personalized itinerary based on your travel dates and interests.
Can I add additional activities after booking my Iceland tour or vacation package?
Yes, in most cases, you can add extra tours or experiences after booking, depending on availability and timing. It’s best to contact us as early as possible to update your booking and secure any additional activities.

You’re in safe hands with our team of local experts
As Iceland’s Leading Travel Agency for eight consecutive years, we pride ourselves on offering personalized service, making us the go-to resource for unforgettable Icelandic adventures.
With most of our team calling Iceland home, we are deeply invested in protecting its natural beauty. This commitment drives us to work closely with local partners, promoting sustainable practices and eco-friendly tourism to preserve Iceland for future generations.
